claim: More tools in agent context window make agents perform worse; LangChain research showed over 100 tools reduces agent effectiveness despite user diversity needs
actionable: When building agent systems, limit default tool sets to 40-50 tools maximum; allow advanced users to opt into additional tools rather than exposing all tools by default
source_title: Scaling GitHub for your Agents — Sam Morrow, GitHub

claim: Tool proliferation problem: users default to all settings rather than configuring tool sets even when elegant solutions for grouping are provided
actionable: When designing agent tools, do not rely on user configuration; bake intelligent defaults into the system rather than exposing configuration options
source_title: Scaling GitHub for your Agents — Sam Morrow, GitHub

claim: Optimizing output tokens from API responses reduces GitHub MCP server context overhead by 75% on list pull requests endpoint alone
actionable: When building MCP servers, aggressively trim output from API responses to only essential fields; avoid returning full object payloads to agents
source_title: Scaling GitHub for your Agents — Sam Morrow, GitHub

claim: Tool success rate improves from baseline to 95%+ by encoding agent intent into tool surface, making 5 API calls server-side instead of round-tripping
actionable: Design agent tools to be intent-aware; bundle multiple API calls into single tool invocations to improve success rates and reduce round trips
source_title: Scaling GitHub for your Agents — Sam Morrow, GitHub

claim: OAuth with PKCE is more secure than static access tokens for agent-system integration; GitHub added PKCE support to improve security posture
actionable: Use OAuth 2.1 with PKCE instead of static tokens for agent integrations; implement step-up OAuth to request scopes dynamically rather than upfront
source_title: Scaling GitHub for your Agents — Sam Morrow, GitHub

claim: Stateless MCP server design with new server instance per request allows horizontal scaling; Redis for session storage tracks client identity for observability
actionable: Build remote MCP servers as stateless services creating new instances per request; use Redis for lightweight session tracking without state affinity
source_title: Scaling GitHub for your Agents — Sam Morrow, GitHub

claim: GitHub MCP server handles 7+ million tool calls per week through optimized architecture; context reduction from initial load is ongoing work
actionable: When designing scalable agent infrastructure, budget for continuous optimization of tool outputs and context usage to handle production load growth
source_title: Scaling GitHub for your Agents — Sam Morrow, GitHub

claim: Prompt injection attacks on agent systems are not unique to GitHub MCP; the lethal trifecta problem applies to all agent setups regardless of tool provider
actionable: When deploying agents, assume prompt injection is possible; design with least-privilege access and consider air-gapping sensitive systems as mitigation
source_title: Scaling GitHub for your Agents — Sam Morrow, GitHub
